description:
You are working as an observability engineer who has just finished improving a “latency-overview” Grafana dashboard.  
    Management wants a simple, machine-readable confirmation that the tuning work has been completed inside the engineering workspace.  
    Perform the following steps from the terminal (they can all be chained into ONE shell command):

    1. Create a new directory named “logs” under “/home/user/observability”.  
       • If “/home/user/observability” does not yet exist, create it as well.  

    2. Inside “/home/user/observability/logs”, create a file called “tuning_status.json”.  
       • The file must contain exactly the following three-field JSON document, including spelling, letter-case, spacing, and final newline:

{
  "dashboard": "latency-overview",
  "tuned": true,
  "timestamp": "2023-09-10T00:00:00Z"
}

    3. The command must succeed silently (i.e., produce no stdout or stderr on success).

Verification:
    • The automated checker will read “/home/user/observability/logs/tuning_status.json”.  
    • It must match the JSON shown above byte-for-byte (including the trailing newline).  
    • It will also confirm that the containing directory exists exactly at “/home/user/observability/logs”.

Hints (do NOT output these as commands, just think through them):
    • A single mkdir -p + printf/redirection chain is sufficient.  
    • Remember to include the trailing newline at the very end of the file.

Your task is complete when that file exists with the exact content and the command that created it produced no output.
truth:
Expected directory path:
        /home/user/observability/logs

    Expected file path:
        /home/user/observability/logs/tuning_status.json

    Expected file contents (with trailing newline):
{
  "dashboard": "latency-overview",
  "tuned": true,
  "timestamp": "2023-09-10T00:00:00Z"
}

    The automated test will:
        1. `test -d /home/user/observability/logs`
        2. `cat /home/user/observability/logs/tuning_status.json` and compare to the exact string above (including final newline).
name: task_000000_98eb1fc4
